working_on: Close Issues #126/#127/#128/#129 — routine-mode doc-drift fixes plus a SKILL/Layer-1 parity validator check, and two MemPalace-probe blind-spot fixes.
declared_scope: Fix #126 (add task_id/target_id to routine eager-claim enumeration; reorder routine close step 11 so diary write precedes audit), #127 (probe_palace.py UNKNOWN-state detection + validate.py soft-warn), #128 (health_check.py wing-spanning SQLite queries), #129 (new skill_layer1_parity_drift validator check + ADR 0089 + ADR 0044 amendment). phase: NA-engine-apparatus
outcome_summary: Closed Issues #126/#127/#128/#129 — the four S-0162 cadence-audit findings — in one interactive build session. #129: new structural-phase skill_layer1_parity_drift validator check (validate_skill_layer1_parity + _SKILL_LAYER1_PAIRS config) + engine ADR 0089 + ADR 0044 present-truth Consequences amendment + MemPalace decision drawer + 15 pytests; dogfood surfaced build-readiness-gate.md missing step 10 (fixed in-context). #126: routine eager-claim task_id/target_id fields enumerated; the canonical close sequence reordered+renumbered 1-15 so the diary write + pushback/lesson capture + activity rollup precede the audit pass (session-shutdown-sequence.md gained the activity-rollup step it had drifted behind its SKILL on). #127: probe_palace.py detects mempalace repair-status status:UNKNOWN; new sibling soft-warn mempalace_hnsw_status_suspect. #128: audit_mempalace() converted to wing-agnostic read-only SQLite queries — the decisions room now reads its true cross-wing count (no false compliance gap), pushback/lesson probe returns 105/144; dead _resolve_mempalace_binary/_run_mempalace removed. Verification: 1479 pytest green, 0 validator hard-fails, ruff+ruff format+mypy --strict clean, cold-review sub-agent found no bugs. The two new soft-warns (#127/#129) fire as designed; mempalace_*_late is the benign plan-mode-then-claim artifact (S-0160); validator_runtime_phase_regression fired on a transient cold-venv-creation structural-phase spike (3534ms) — recent steady-state runs are 445-553ms, near the 500ms target, so the new structural-phase check did not meaningfully regress timing. One engine ADR (0089). lesson drawer drawer_paideia_lessons_81f948119486e559e4dee743.

scope_delivery.explanation: Yes — all four issues closed end-to-end. #126 part 2 expanded beyond a literal 'reorder' (the Layer-1 doc had drifted behind its SKILL on multiple axes, so the reorder required reconciling the doc to current SKILL content) — delivered more thoroughly than the plan's literal wording, not descoped. One in-band addition: build-readiness-gate.md step 10, surfaced by the new #129 check and fixed in-context per default-to-fix-in-context. cold-review pass (Python/engine): 3 file(s), all match contract.

side_discoveries.reasoning: The phrase 'the parity drift the #129 check flagged' describes the new skill_layer1_parity_drift check's behavior in-narrative — the session-shutdown-sequence parity gap it surfaced was reconciled in the same commit. Not a deferred discovery.
'deferred-re-audit entry' is the literal name of a documented classification bucket / subsection in tools-validate-interpretation.md, not a deferral of work.

The skill_layer1_parity_drift check fires on session-build-lifecycle, which is the open Issue #125's instance — already tracked, out of S-0163's scope. The soft-warn firing on it is the mechanism working as intended; no new action, #125 owns the fix.

'deferred-re-audit entry' is the literal name of a documented classification subsection in tools-validate-interpretation.md (the #127 commit added the mempalace_hnsw_status_suspect entry there), not a deferral of work — same as the 6ca90c2 marker.
